rulings.Case Title: NEXTG NETWORKS OF CALIFORNIA, INC., A DELAWARE VS. THE CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Respondent The City And County Of San Francisco, The City And County Of San Francisco Department, The City And County Of San Francisco Board Of Motion To Consolidate Actions Of Case(S) Cpf-12-512623 With Case Cpf-11-511369
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Friday, September 20, 2013, Line 18, Respondent The City And County Of San Francisco, The City And County Of San Francisco Department, The City And County Of San Francisco Board Of Motion To Consolidate Actions Of Case(S) Cpf-12-512623 With Case Cpf-11-511369 The motion to consolidate is denied. The two actions involve two separate administrative actions involving two separate permits. Respondents have not shown that the two cases concern a common question of law or fact such that judicial economy would be served by consolidating them. The Court orders Petitioner to calendar the hearing on it writ in case #511369 by December 31, 2013, or show cause why that is not feasible. The parties should note that in case 512623 the Court does not have the administrative record, a courtesy copy of the petition or a courtesy copy of the opposition brief. See Local Rule 2.6B. The parties must provide these documents to Department 302 by no later than September 23, 2013 at 4:00 p.m. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/mjm

rulings.Case Title: SHAWN CHOY et al VS. CENTURY SURETY COMPANY, AN OHIO CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Discovery Responses And For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, September 20, 2013, Line 1: Plaintiffs SHAWN CHOY'S and KAYLA CHA'S Motion to Compel Discovery Responses and for Monetary Sanctions (Part 1 of 2:) Pro Tem Judge Charles Geerhart, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff's RPD Set 1: RPD 16: Century says it has provided all responsive writings. The Mayer Decl., 8, suggests that there may be additional training materials used in presentations, even if written materials are not distributed. To the extent any additional training materials used in California training sessions exist, they should be produced. RPD 17: Century says it has provided all responsive writings. To the extent any additional materials showing changes in claims handling guidelines in California exist, they should be produced. RPD 18: Even if the adjusters who handled this claim were attorneys, the materials sought could lead to the discovery of admissible evidence on the subject of Century's standards for claim handling. Century is ordered to produce responsive documents, if any exist. RPD 19: Even if the adjusters who handled this claim were attorneys, the materials sought could lead to the discovery of admissible evidence on the subject of Century's pattern of compliance with insurance regulations. Century is ordered to produce responsive documents limited to period 2008-2011, if any exist. (Continued to Part 2 of 2) =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: SHAWN CHOY et al VS. CENTURY SURETY COMPANY, AN OHIO CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiffs Shawn Choy'S And Kayla Cha'S Motion To Compel Discovery Responses And For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, September 20, 2013, Line 1: Plaintiffs SHAWN CHOY'S and KAYLA CHA'S Motion to Compel Discovery Responses and for Monetary Sanctions (Part 2 of 2:) RPD 20: Even if the adjusters who handled this claim were attorneys, the materials sought could lead to the discovery of admissible evidence on the subject of Century's pattern of compliance with insurance regulations. Century is ordered to produce responsive documents limited to period 2008-2011, if any exist. RPD 21: The materials sought could lead to the discovery of admissible evidence on the subject of Century's pattern of compliance with insurance regulations. Century is ordered to produce responsive documents limited to period 2008-2011, if any exist. RPD 22: The materials sought could lead to the discovery of admissible evidence on the subject of Century's pattern of compliance with insurance regulations. Century is ordered to produce responsive documents limited to period 2008-2011, if any exist. RPD 23: The materials sought could lead to the discovery of admissible evidence on the subject of Century's pattern of compliance with insurance regulations. Century is ordered to produce responsive documents limited to period 2008-2011, if any exist. RPD 24: The materials sought could lead to the discovery of admissible evidence on the subject of Century's pattern of compliance with insurance regulations. Century is ordered to produce responsive documents limited to period 2008-2011, if any exist. RPD 28: Even if the adjusters who handled this claim were attorneys, they may have received some training, and the materials sought could lead to the discovery of admissible evidence on the subject of Century's standards for claim handling. Century is ordered to produce responsive documents, if any exist. RPD 30: Century says it has provided all responsive writings. The Mayer Decl., 8, suggests that there may be additional training materials used in presentations, even if written materials are not distributed. To the extent any additional training materials used in California training sessions exist, they should be produced. RPD 45: The materials sought could lead to the discovery of admissible evidence on the subject of Century's claims handling practices in case involving 3rd party assaults. Century is ordered to produce coverage and acceptance letters for California claims limited to the period from 2008-2011. Names in these letters may be redacted to protect 3rd party privacy. RPD 46: The materials sought could lead to the discovery of admissible evidence on the subject of how Century construed its policy language. If responsive documents exist, they should be produced. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to cgeerhart@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: URS CORPORATION DBA URS CORPORATION AMERICAS, A VS. UNIVERSAL MOLDING EXTRUSION COMPANY, INC. DBA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Friday, September 20, 2013, Line 9, PLAINTIFF URS CORPORATION DBA URS CORPORATION AMERICAS, A Notice Of Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint The motion is granted in part. Plaintiff has leave to amend its claim for damages from $326,874.64 to $346,931.77. The remainder of the motion is denied. The motion is denied as to adding UMC as a defendant. Allowing amendment at this time would be prejudicial. The trial date would need to be continued and discovery re-opened. Plaintiff may seek to add UMC as an alter ego judgment debtor after obtaining judgment against the existing defendant. rulings.Case Title: JOHN LIGGETT VS. SAUSE BROS. INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Friday, September 20, 2013, Line 13, PLAINTIFF JOHN LIGGETT DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT Sustain demurrer, without leave to amend as to both causes of action. The first cause of action for declaratory relief raises a question of fact that will necessarily be settled in the underlying Complaint. It fails to state a claim under CCP §430.10(e) and CCP §§1060 and 1061. The second cause of action for credit/set off is an affirmative defense that has already been raised in response to the original Complaint. (See Third Affirmative Defense.) CCP 430.10(e). Cross-Complainant does not oppose the motion to strike the request for attorney fees. However, in light of the ruling on the demurrer, the motion to strike is denied as moot. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested.=302/mjm

rulings.Case Title: RAYMOND E. FROST VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Pltfs' State Law Causes Of Action Pursuant To Cccp 425.16
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Friday, September 20, 2013, Line 14, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, LORENZO DONATI, DYANA LECHUGA Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Pltfs' State Law Causes Of Action Pursuant To Cccp 425.16 Defendants' Special Motion to Strike Plaintiff's State Law Causes of Action Pursuant to CCP § 425.16 is granted as to all causes of action. All causes of action arise from protected activity under the anti-SLAPP statute. Plaintiff concedes the City should not be a defendant and that its ratification claim will not succeed. Plaintiff has failed to show a probability of prevailing on the first, second, and fourth causes of action because the causes of action are barred by the litigation privilege. See Civil Code §47(b). Plaintiff has failed to show a probability of prevailing on the third cause of action for malicious prosecution because it is barred by governmental immunity under Gov. Code § 821.6. In the alternative, even if Defendants were not immune, Plaintiff fails to show a probability of success on the merits, since Plaintiff cannot establish that Defendant lacked probable cause. Defendants' Request for Judicial Notice is granted. Plaintiff's request to amend the complaint--made in the opposition to this motion--is denied. Pursuant to CCP Section 425.16(c ), the Court awards reasonable attorneys fees to Defendants in the amount of $15,000. The Court finds that the $375 per hour rate is reasonable. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/mjm

If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Granted, no opposition. Sanctions awarded in amount of $800 against defendant. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to cgeerhart@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)
